summary_one_line: Input must complete a <!DOCTYPE ... [ ... ]> internal subset so xmlParseLookupInternalSubset finds its closing delimiter and returns nonzero.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A DTD whose internal subset opens with '<!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "..."' followed by a '[' and is then driven far enough that xmlParseLookupInternalSubset locates the closing ']' / '>' delimiter of the subset; the parser must be in XML_PARSER_DTD state with a lookahead-complete internal subset, not merely an opened-but-unterminated one.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog seeds carry well-formed DTD prologs ('<?xml version="1.0"?>' then '<!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "dtds/1...' / 'dtd://') and build up richer subset bodies (xlink namespace strings, multiple declarations), so the buffered lookahead reaches the internal-subset terminator and xmlParseLookupInternalSubset returns true, taking the false branch into xmlParseInternalSubset.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog seeds stall on near-miss DTD bytes ('dtds~1', 'S[STEM', 'DOCTYPE\n', 'dt.s=1') that either corrupt the DOCTYPE keyword or never complete the internal subset, so xmlParseLookupInternalSubset keeps returning 0 (true branch, goto done) and the parser re-enters the loop without progressing.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance gradient feedback on top of the I2S that both fuzzers already share; the gradient rewards inputs that get incrementally closer to satisfying the byte-comparison-driven lookahead inside xmlParseLookupInternalSubset, giving the winner a smooth path to a complete internal subset that cmplog (edge-bucket feedback only) cannot climb.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch is the false side of xmlParseLookupInternalSubset in the XML_PARSER_DTD state.
The branch sits inside the XML_PARSER_DTD switch arm whose false side proceeds into xmlParseInternalSubset.
Taking the true branch goes to done (block); the false branch parses the internal subset (resolve).
Winner seeds contain complete XML/DTD prologs ending in a SYSTEM DOCTYPE that opens an internal subset.
A winner seed carries richer internal-subset content including xlink namespace declarations.
Loser seeds carry near-miss DOCTYPE/SYSTEM bytes that corrupt the keyword or never complete the subset.
Another loser seed breaks the DOCTYPE keyword with an embedded newline.
value_profile_cmplog feedback includes CMP_MAP gradient buckets that cmplog lacks.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: per-execution CMP buffer (`CmpLogObserver`), and CMP_MAP gradient buckets.

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog feedback is the same edge-bucket signal as naive, with no CMP_MAP gradient.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
**Feedback**: same edge-bucket signal as naive. The CMP buffer is
consumed by the mutator, not by feedback.

evidence_trail.claim: The only technique delta between the two involved fuzzers is value_profile.
The divergence at L12008 is strong: winner takes the false (resolving) side while loser is pinned on the true side.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is CMP_MAP gradient (value_profile feedback), not I2SRandReplace, so the mandatory I2S lineage check does not apply; both involved fuzzers already share I2SRandReplace, so I2S substitution cannot explain the divergence. The distinguishing signal is value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback, which is a feedback-side mechanism not recorded as a per-seed mutation_op and therefore not lineage-verifiable; evidence rests on the source CMP structure of xmlParseLookupInternalSubset plus the seed-byte progression toward a complete internal subset.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with a lookahead-gated internal-subset terminator showing cmplog resolving the false branch as fast as value_profile_cmplog (no gradient advantage), which would mean CMP_MAP gradient is not the differentiator.

weakest_evidence_point: That value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient specifically guides completion of the internal-subset lookahead is inferred from feedback-mechanism reasoning plus seed-byte progression, not directly observed in lineage since gradient feedback leaves no per-seed mutation_op trace.
